characters.name: Gabriel Betteredge
characters.description: Elderly house steward at the Verinder estate, digressive narrator who consults Robinson Crusoe for guidance in all matters.

characters.name: Lady Verinder
characters.description: Mistress of the house and Rachel's mother, Betteredge's employer.

characters.name: Rachel Verinder
characters.description: Lady Verinder's daughter, young woman who will receive the Moonstone diamond for her birthday.

characters.name: Franklin Blake
characters.description: Young gentleman who brings the Moonstone from London, cosmopolitan and multilingual, returns to the Verinder estate after years abroad.

characters.name: Penelope
characters.description: Betteredge's daughter, a servant at the Verinder house.

characters.name: The three Indian jugglers
characters.description: Three mysterious foreigners lurking near the estate, connected to the Moonstone diamond and its history in India.

voice_profile.tone: chatty and digressive with Robinson Crusoe references, Victorian formal prose

settings.name: Lady Verinder's house
settings.description: Yorkshire country house where the Verinder family lives, the primary setting.

settings.name: Seringapatam
settings.description: Indian city where the Moonstone was originally stolen during the siege.

plot_threads.thread: The arrival of the Moonstone diamond and its cursed history from India
Indians Brahmins priests guardians seeking recover diamond Moonstone stolen
Moonstone diamond lost stolen Rachel birthday Verinder house twelve hours

world_rules: The Moonstone diamond carries a curse from its origins in India, guarded by Brahmin priests
Victorian social hierarchy — the narrative is told from the servants' perspective
Family inheritance customs and the obligations that come with bequests
